title: TCP Connections, Accept Loops, Half-Closes, Deadlines, and Failure · Execution Trace: Read Write Deadlines
summary: A raw server treats TCP as messages, leaks accepted connections, uses no deadlines, retries ambiguous writes, and cannot distinguish peer close, timeout, reset, or local shutdown. Planned artifact: Add and verify the read write deadlines increment in bounded TCP connection lifecycle state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Set and refresh absolute read and write deadlines by protocol phase and classify timeout evidence without assuming cancellation.
Assign listener, accepted connection, goroutine, buffer, deadline, and close ownership with bounded accept-error handling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Read Write Deadlines · Theory Read Write Deadlines
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 1464 in a archival client parsing incomplete close-delimited responses. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify connection, deadline, completion, and close state without inventing message boundaries; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then saturate one stream window while another remains independently serviceable. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Set and refresh absolute read and write deadlines by protocol phase and classify timeout evidence without assuming cancellation.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Read Write Deadlines contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: Inspect Read Write Deadlines evidence · Theory Read Write Deadlines
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 1466 in a field-normalizing cache with an independent origin parser. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify connection, deadline, completion, and close state without inventing message boundaries; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then block extra QPACK streams and fall back without removing HTTP/2 availability. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Set and refresh absolute read and write deadlines by protocol phase and classify timeout evidence without assuming cancellation.
